title: Visualizing Best Response Functions in a 2-Player Game
description: A graph showing Player 2's strategy on the x-axis and Player 1's payoff on the y-axis. Player 1's Best Response Function is plotted, showing the strategy that maximizes their payoff for any given strategy of Player 2.
elements: Player 1's Payoff Curve for a fixed own strategy
Point marking the Maximum Payoff (Best Response) for a given s2
Line representing Player 1's Best Response Function (BR1(s2))
relationships: The payoff curve shows how Player 1's payoff changes against Player 2's strategy while Player 1's strategy is fixed.
The maximum point on the payoff curve identifies the Best Response for that specific situation.
The Best Response Function (BR1) is formed by connecting the maximum payoff points for every possible strategy of Player 2.
